name: sumerian-belt-wrestlers-khafajah
description: A cast copper figurine from the Nintu temple at Khafajah depicts two belt-wrestlers, among the oldest sculptural images of sport.

name: paso-de-la-amada-ballcourt
description: The earliest known formal ballcourt was built at Paso de la Amada in Chiapas, Mexico, launching the Mesoamerican ballgame.

name: wrestling-pentathlon-added-708-bce
description: Wrestling and the five-event pentathlon joined the ancient Olympic program at Olympia, broadening it beyond footraces.

name: boxing-added-olympics-688-bce
description: Boxing, or pygmachia, was added to the ancient Olympic Games at Olympia, with fighters binding their hands in leather thongs.

name: tethrippon-chariot-race-680-bce
description: The four-horse chariot race, the tethrippon, debuted at Olympia as the ancient Games' first equestrian event.

name: circus-maximus-founded-tarquinius
description: Rome's first permanent chariot-racing arena took shape under the Etruscan king Tarquinius Priscus in the Palatine valley.

name: first-great-panathenaic-games-566bce
description: Pisistratus reorganized Athens' festival of Athena into the Great Panathenaia, awarding victors prize amphorae of sacred olive oil.

name: milo-croton-wrestling-reign-532-bce
description: Milo of Croton won the first of five straight Olympic men's wrestling crowns, becoming antiquity's most famous athlete.

name: theagenes-thasos-olympic-boxing-480bce
description: Theagenes of Thasos won the Olympic boxing crown, part of a legendary career later credited with some 1,400 combat victories.

name: diagoras-rhodes-carried-by-sons-448bce
description: After his sons won Olympic boxing and pankration titles, they carried the aged champion Diagoras of Rhodes around the stadium.

name: leonidas-rhodes-twelve-crowns-152-bce
description: Leonidas of Rhodes finished a fourth Olympiad sweep of three footraces, amassing a record twelve individual crowns.

name: pompeii-amphitheatre-built-70-bce
description: Magistrates Valgus and Porcius built the amphitheatre at Pompeii, the oldest surviving Roman stone arena for gladiatorial games.

name: nero-olympic-chariot-victory-67ad
description: Emperor Nero fell from his ten-horse chariot at Olympia yet was declared the winner, later stripped from the victor lists.

name: colosseum-inaugural-games-80-ce
description: Emperor Titus opened the Colosseum with 100 days of gladiatorial fights, wild-beast hunts, and staged spectacles in Rome.

name: diocles-charioteer-retires-146
description: Charioteer Gaius Appuleius Diocles retired after winning 35,863,120 sesterces, likely the best-paid athlete ever.

name: constantinople-hippodrome-completed-330
description: Constantine completed the Hippodrome of Constantinople, the vast chariot arena at the heart of Byzantine public life.

name: ancient-olympics-abolished-393-ce
description: Emperor Theodosius I's ban on pagan festivals is traditionally credited with ending the ancient Olympic Games.

name: nika-riots-constantinople-532-ce
description: Constantinople's Blue and Green chariot factions united in revolt against Justinian, killing tens of thousands and burning the city.

name: kemari-earliest-record
description: Japan's Nihon Shoki records Prince Naka no Oe and Nakatomi no Kamatari meeting at a kemari football game.

name: sechie-zumo-imperial-court
description: Emperor Shomu staged formal palace sumo tournaments, launching the sechie-zumo court ritual in Nara Japan.

name: great-ballcourt-chichen-itza-864
description: The Maya dedicated the Great Ballcourt at Chichen Itza, the largest ballcourt in Mesoamerica at 168 metres long.

name: first-recorded-tournament-valenciennes-1114
description: The word 'tournament' first appears in Count Baldwin III of Hainaut's 1114 peace charter for the town of Valenciennes.

name: fitzstephen-football-1174
description: William Fitzstephen described London youths playing a Shrovetide ball game, an early written record of football.

name: bone-skate-skating-london-1174
description: Londoners strapped animal-bone skates to their feet and slid across frozen Moorfields, per Fitzstephen's account.

name: yabusame-established-yoritomo
description: Shogun Minamoto no Yoritomo instituted yabusame horseback archery as a Shinto rite at Tsurugaoka Hachimangu.

name: genghis-khan-stele-archery
description: A Mongolian stele recorded Yesungge's archery shot of over 500 meters at a gathering of Genghis Khan's nobles.

name: colf-loenen-match-1297
description: A colf match was reportedly played at Loenen aan de Vecht, an early ancestor of golf and the modern Dutch kolf.

name: southampton-oldest-bowling-green-1299
description: The Old Bowling Green in Southampton, England, hosted its first recorded game of bowls in 1299 and is still played on today.

name: edward-ii-bans-football-london-1314
friendly_name: Edward II Bans Football in London
description: King Edward II banned football within the City of London in 1314, citing the tumult and brawling caused by mob football games.

name: kirkpinar-oil-wrestling-founded
description: The Ottoman Kırkpınar tournament in Edirne began, becoming the world's oldest continuous sanctioned sport.

name: combat-of-the-thirty-1351
description: Thirty champions a side fought a celebrated arranged combat between Josselin and Ploermel in the Breton War of Succession.

name: edward-iii-archery-law-1363
description: King Edward III's 1363 decree ordered every able-bodied Englishman to practise archery on Sundays and forbade rival sports.

name: scotland-football-act-1424
description: Scotland's Parliament under King James I passed the Football Act of 1424, fining anyone caught playing football fourpence.

name: stoolball-first-reference-1450
description: A manual for parish priests condemned playing 'stoolball' in churchyards, the game's first known written mention.

name: aztec-ballgame-for-charles-v
description: Hernan Cortes took Aztec ballplayers to Spain, where they performed the Mesoamerican rubber-ball game before Emperor Charles V.

name: calcio-fiorentino-siege-match-1530
description: Florentines defiantly played calcio in the Piazza Santa Croce in 1530 while the city was under siege by Emperor Charles V's troops.

name: falkland-oldest-real-tennis-court-1539
description: King James V built the real tennis court at Falkland Palace, Scotland, in 1539, now the oldest surviving court in the world.

name: chester-races-roodee
description: Horse racing began at Chester's Roodee course in 1539, recognised by Guinness as the oldest racecourse still in operation.

name: first-pallone-rules-1555
description: Antonio Scaino published the first formal rules for pallone col bracciale, Italy's popular arm-guard ball game.

name: henry-ii-france-jousting-death-1559
description: King Henry II of France was fatally wounded when a lance splinter pierced his eye during a Paris joust in 1559.

name: bardi-calcio-fiorentino-rules-1580
description: Florentine count Giovanni de' Bardi published the first codified rules of calcio fiorentino, set out in 33 articles, in 1580.

name: naqsh-e-jahan-polo-ground
description: Shah Abbas laid out Isfahan's Naqsh-e Jahan Square as a polo field; its stone goalposts still stand today.

name: earliest-cricket-reference-guildford
description: A 1598 court case in Guildford recorded testimony that cricket, or 'creckett', had been played there by schoolboys around 1550.

name: newmarket-town-plate-founded
description: King Charles II set out the rules for the Newmarket Town Plate in 1665, one of the first horse races run under a formal code.

name: edo-professional-sumo-tomioka
description: Fifteen rikishi including Ikazuchi Gondayu won shogunal permits for kanjin sumo at Tomioka Hachiman, marking pro sumo's birth.

name: james-figg-first-boxing-champion
description: James Figg opened his London amphitheatre in 1719 and became recognised as England's first bare-knuckle boxing champion.

name: first-rules-of-golf-leith
description: The Gentlemen Golfers of Edinburgh drew up golf's first 13 written rules in 1744 for a competition on Leith Links.

name: laws-of-cricket-codified-1744
description: The London Cricket Club at the Artillery Ground drew up the earliest known code of the Laws of Cricket, fixing the 22-yard pitch.

name: society-of-st-andrews-golfers
description: Formed in 1754, the Society of St Andrews Golfers later became the Royal and Ancient, golf's global rule-making authority.

name: eclipse-retires-unbeaten-1770
description: The English thoroughbred Eclipse retired in 1770 unbeaten in 18 races and became an ancestor of most modern racehorses.

name: first-st-leger-stakes-1776
description: The St Leger, run at Doncaster and the oldest of Britain's five Classics, held its first running in 1776.

name: jahn-first-turnplatz-1811
description: Friedrich Ludwig Jahn opened the first open-air Turnplatz gymnasium in Berlin's Hasenheide, launching the German Turnen movement.

name: william-webb-ellis-legend
description: By legend, schoolboy William Webb Ellis picked up the ball and ran during a game at Rugby School, birthing rugby.

name: first-oxford-cambridge-boat-race
description: Oxford beat Cambridge at Henley-on-Thames in the first University Boat Race, launching one of sport's oldest rivalries.

name: first-henley-regatta
description: The inaugural Henley Regatta was held on the Thames at Henley-on-Thames, later gaining royal patronage from Prince Albert in 1851.

name: first-grand-national-1839
description: Lottery won the first official Grand National steeplechase at Aintree near Liverpool in 1839.

name: first-baseball-game-hoboken-1846
description: The Knickerbockers lost the first recorded baseball game under written rules to the New York Nine at Elysian Fields, Hoboken.

name: first-americas-cup-race
description: The schooner America won the Royal Yacht Squadron's race around the Isle of Wight, originating the America's Cup trophy.

name: first-australian-rules-match-1858
description: Scotch College and Melbourne Grammar played the first recorded game of what became Australian rules football.

name: aussie-rules-first-laws-1859
description: Tom Wills and colleagues drafted the ten original Rules of the Melbourne Football Club, founding Australian rules football.

name: first-open-championship-1860
description: Willie Park Sr won the inaugural Open at Prestwick in Scotland, beating Tom Morris Sr among a field of eight golfers.

name: first-melbourne-cup-1861
description: Archer won the inaugural Melbourne Cup at Flemington in 1861, launching Australia's race that stops a nation.

name: overarm-bowling-legalized-1864
description: In 1864 overarm bowling was made legal, transforming the sport by allowing bowlers far more pace, bounce and variation.

name: first-bicycle-race-saint-cloud-1868
description: On 31 May 1868 Englishman James Moore won the first documented bicycle race, a 1,200 m sprint in the Parc de Saint-Cloud, Paris.

name: first-college-football-game-1869
description: In New Brunswick, Rutgers beat Princeton 6-4 in the first intercollegiate American football game, played under rugby-like rules.

name: cincinnati-red-stockings-first-pro-team-1869
description: Harry Wright's Cincinnati Red Stockings became baseball's first openly all-salaried team, touring the nation undefeated.

name: rugby-football-union-founded-1871
description: Representatives of 21 clubs met at London's Pall Mall Restaurant to found the Rugby Football Union and codify its laws.

name: first-rugby-international-scotland-england
description: Scotland beat England at Raeburn Place in Edinburgh in the world's first international rugby match, played 20-a-side.

name: first-football-international-1872
description: Scotland and England drew 0-0 at Hamilton Crescent in Glasgow in the first officially recognised international match.

name: wingfield-patents-lawn-tennis
description: Major Walter Clopton Wingfield patented his portable court game 'Sphairistike' in England, launching lawn tennis.

name: webb-first-english-channel-swim-1875
description: Captain Matthew Webb became the first person to swim the English Channel unaided, from Dover to Calais.

name: snooker-invented-chamberlain-india
description: British officer Neville Chamberlain devised snooker at an army mess in Jabalpur, India, blending pyramids and black pool.

name: first-indoor-hockey-game-1875
description: The first organized indoor ice hockey game was played at Montreal's Victoria Skating Rink using a flat wooden puck.

name: national-league-founded-1876
description: William Hulbert founded the National League of Professional Base Ball Clubs, still one of Major League Baseball's two leagues.

name: kincsem-undefeated-54-races
description: Hungarian mare Kincsem retired in 1879 having won all 54 of her races, the longest unbeaten record in the sport.

name: first-us-national-tennis-championship
description: The inaugural U.S. National Championship was played at the Newport Casino in Rhode Island, the event that became the US Open.

name: fig-gymnastics-federation-founded-1881
description: The Federation Internationale de Gymnastique was founded in Liege, Belgium, the world's oldest international sports federation.

name: the-ashes-born-1882
description: After Australia's first Test win on English soil at The Oval, a mock obituary in the Sporting Times gave birth to the Ashes.

name: jigoro-kano-founds-judo-1882
description: Jigoro Kano founded Kodokan judo in Tokyo, opening his first dojo at Eishoji Temple with nine students.

name: axel-paulsen-invents-axel-jump
description: Norwegian Axel Paulsen debuted his forward-takeoff jump in Vienna, creating the axel, figure skating's oldest jump.

name: first-home-nations-championship
description: England, Scotland, Ireland and Wales contested the first Home Nations Championship, ancestor of the Six Nations.

name: maud-watson-first-wimbledon-ladies-1884
description: Maud Watson beat her sister Lilian in 1884 to win Wimbledon's first Ladies' Singles, tennis's first women's major title.

name: gaelic-athletic-association-founded-1884
description: Michael Cusack and others founded the GAA at Hayes' Hotel in Thurles to revive hurling and Gaelic football in Ireland.

name: softball-invented-1887
description: George Hancock devised the first indoor game of softball at Chicago's Farragut Boat Club on Thanksgiving Day.

name: football-league-founded-1888
description: Aston Villa's William McGregor founded the Football League with twelve clubs, the world's first organised football league.

name: first-british-isles-rugby-tour
description: A British Isles team toured Australia and New Zealand, the pioneering tour that became the British and Irish Lions.

name: county-championship-founded-1890
description: The English County Championship was officially constituted in 1890, with Surrey crowned the inaugural champions.

name: first-french-championships-1891
description: The first French Championships were held in Paris as a men's interclub event, the tournament that would become the French Open.

name: naismith-basketball-rules-published
description: James Naismith's original 13 rules of basketball were published in the Springfield YMCA journal The Triangle in January 1892.

name: first-gloved-heavyweight-title-1892
description: James J. Corbett knocked out John L. Sullivan in New Orleans to win boxing's first gloved world heavyweight title.

name: first-liege-bastogne-liege-1892
description: Léon Houa won the inaugural Liège-Bastogne-Liège in 1892, the race that became the oldest of cycling's five Monuments.

name: desgrange-first-hour-record-1893
description: Henri Desgrange rode 35.325 km in Paris on 11 May 1893 to set the first officially recognised cycling hour record.

name: first-motor-race-paris-rouen-1894
description: Le Petit Journal's Paris-to-Rouen contest in France is widely regarded as the world's first competitive motor race.

name: great-schism-northern-union-1895
description: Twenty-one northern clubs met at Huddersfield's George Hotel and broke from the RFU, founding rugby league's forerunner.

name: first-us-open-golf-1895
description: Englishman Horace Rawlins won the inaugural U.S. Open at Newport Golf Club in Rhode Island, staged by the USGA.

name: first-modern-olympics-athens-1896
description: The first modern Olympic Games opened in Athens, with 241 male athletes from 14 nations competing.

name: cycling-olympic-debut-athens-1896
description: Cycling featured at the first modern Olympics in Athens, with six track and road events at the Neo Phaliron Velodrome.

name: first-paris-roubaix-1896-fischer
description: Germany's Josef Fischer won the inaugural Paris-Roubaix, the 280 km classic later nicknamed the Hell of the North.

name: hajos-first-olympic-swim-1896
description: Hungary's Alfred Hajos won the 100m freestyle at the 1896 Athens Games, the first Olympic swimming title ever awarded.

name: first-world-figure-skating-champs-1896
description: The inaugural World Figure Skating Championships were held in St. Petersburg and won by Germany's Gilbert Fuchs.

name: first-all-england-badminton-1899
description: The inaugural All England Badminton Championships were staged in London, becoming the sport's oldest and most prestigious event.

name: first-davis-cup-tennis
description: The United States beat the British Isles in Boston in the first international team tennis competition, founded by Dwight Davis.

name: uci-founded-1900-paris
description: The Union Cycliste Internationale, cycling's world governing body, was founded in Paris by five national federations.

name: women-first-compete-olympics-1900
description: Women competed at the Olympics for the first time in Paris, where Charlotte Cooper became the first female champion.

name: rowing-olympic-debut-1900
description: Rowing was contested at the Paris Olympics after the 1896 races were cancelled by weather, marking its first Olympic appearance.

name: first-bodybuilding-contest-1901
description: Eugen Sandow staged 'The Great Competition' at London's Royal Albert Hall, regarded as the first modern bodybuilding show.

name: american-league-founded-1901
description: The American League declared itself a major league, launching a rivalry that would create modern Major League Baseball.

name: first-artistic-gymnastics-worlds-1903
description: The first Artistic Gymnastics World Championships were held in Antwerp, contested as a men's team event by four European nations.

name: original-all-blacks-tour
description: New Zealand's Original All Blacks toured Britain, winning 34 of 35 games and cementing their black-jersey mystique.

name: first-australasian-championships-1905
description: The first Australasian Championships were played in Melbourne, the tournament that later became the Australian Open.

name: forward-pass-legalized-1906
description: College rules makers legalized the forward pass to open up play and curb the deadly mass formations causing fatalities.

name: first-french-grand-prix-1906
description: The Automobile Club de France ran the first race titled Grand Prix on closed roads near Le Mans, won by Ferenc Szisz.

name: rugby-league-adopts-thirteen
description: The Northern Union cut teams from 15 to 13 players and introduced the play-the-ball, defining rugby league's style.

name: first-isle-of-man-tt-1907
description: The first Isle of Man Tourist Trophy motorcycle race was held, launching what became road racing's most famous event.

name: first-milan-san-remo-1907
description: The inaugural Milan-San Remo, cycling's longest Monument, was held in 1907 and won by Frenchman Lucien Petit-Breton.

name: fina-founded-1908
description: Eight national federations founded FINA during the London Olympics to govern swimming, diving and water polo.

name: jack-johnson-wins-world-title
friendly_name: Jack Johnson Wins the World Title
description: Jack Johnson defeated Tommy Burns in Sydney to become the first Black world heavyweight boxing champion.

name: iihf-founded-1908
description: Belgium, France, Great Britain and Switzerland founded the International Ice Hockey Federation in Paris.

name: first-giro-ditalia-1909-ganna
description: Luigi Ganna won the first Giro d'Italia, an eight-stage race launched by La Gazzetta dello Sport starting in Milan.

name: first-grey-cup
description: The University of Toronto won the inaugural Grey Cup, the trophy for Canada's Dominion football championship donated by Earl Grey.

name: france-joins-five-nations
description: France joined the Home Nations Championship, expanding it into the Five Nations rugby union tournament.

name: first-monte-carlo-rally-1911
description: Henri Rougier won the first Monte Carlo Rally, driving a Turcat-Méry to Monaco from one of eleven European start cities.

name: iaaf-founded-stockholm-1912
description: Delegates from 17 national federations met after the Stockholm Olympics to found the IAAF, athletics' world governing body.

name: first-olympic-decathlon-thorpe
description: The ten-event decathlon debuted at the Stockholm Olympics, won by American Jim Thorpe with a score that stood for years.

name: equestrian-olympic-debut-1912
description: Dressage, eventing and show jumping debuted as Olympic sports at the 1912 Stockholm Games, open only to military officers.

name: olympic-rings-designed-1913
description: Pierre de Coubertin designed the five interlocking Olympic rings, symbolizing the union of the world's continents.

name: ouimet-us-open-upset-1913
description: Amateur Francis Ouimet beat British stars Harry Vardon and Ted Ray in a playoff to win the U.S. Open at Brookline.

name: first-pga-championship-1916
description: Jim Barnes won the first PGA Championship at Siwanoy Country Club the same year the PGA of America was founded.

name: first-copa-america-1916
description: The South American Championship, later Copa América, was first held, becoming football's oldest continental tournament.

name: nfl-founded-canton-1920
description: The American Professional Football Association, renamed the NFL in 1922, formed in Ralph Hay's Canton, Ohio auto showroom.

name: negro-national-league-founded-1920
description: Rube Foster founded the Negro National League in Kansas City, the first lasting professional league for Black ballplayers.

name: first-prix-arc-de-triomphe
friendly_name: First Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe
description: The Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe was first run at Longchamp in Paris in 1920, won by Comrade, becoming Europe's premier race.

name: first-comrades-marathon-1921
description: Bill Rowan won the first Comrades Marathon in South Africa, now the world's oldest and largest ultramarathon.

name: rugby-football-league-named
description: The Northern Union renamed itself the Rugby Football League, giving the breakaway code its enduring name.

name: funakoshi-brings-karate-to-japan
description: Gichin Funakoshi gave the first public demonstration of Okinawan karate in mainland Japan, spreading the art nationwide.

name: weissmuller-sub-minute-1922
description: Johnny Weissmuller became the first man to swim 100m freestyle under one minute, clocking 58.6 seconds.

name: first-walker-cup-1922
description: The first Walker Cup pitted amateur teams from the United States and Great Britain, with the U.S. winning 8-4.

name: first-24-hours-le-mans-1923
description: André Lagache and René Léonard won the inaugural 24 Hours of Le Mans endurance race in a Chenard-Walcker in France.

name: paavo-nurmi-paris-golds-1924
description: Finland's Paavo Nurmi, the Flying Finn, won five gold medals at the 1924 Paris Olympics, twice within an hour.

name: first-deaflympics-paris-1924
description: The first International Silent Games for deaf athletes, later renamed the Deaflympics, were held in Paris, France.

name: offside-law-changed-1925
description: The offside law was amended to require two defenders instead of three, boosting goalscoring across the game.

name: red-grange-turns-pro
description: Star halfback Red Grange signed with the Chicago Bears, drawing huge crowds and boosting the credibility of pro football.

name: harlem-globetrotters-founded
description: Abe Saperstein founded the Harlem Globetrotters, an all-Black touring exhibition team, in Chicago in 1926.

name: ederle-first-woman-channel-swim-1926
description: American Gertrude Ederle became the first woman to swim the English Channel, beating the men's record.

name: first-world-table-tennis-champs-1926
description: London hosted the first World Table Tennis Championships as the International Table Tennis Federation was founded the same year.

name: first-ryder-cup-1927
description: The United States, captained by Walter Hagen, beat Great Britain at Worcester Country Club in the first Ryder Cup.

name: nurburgring-opens-1927
description: The Nurburgring opened in Germany's Eifel mountains, its 22 km Nordschleife later nicknamed 'The Green Hell'.

name: first-pro-road-worlds-1927
description: Italy's Alfredo Binda won the first professional UCI Road World Championship at the Nürburgring, earning the first rainbow jersey.

name: women-first-olympic-athletics-1928
description: Women competed in Olympic track and field for the first time at Amsterdam, where Lina Radke won the debut 800m in a world record.

name: sonja-henie-first-olympic-gold
description: Norway's Sonja Henie won her first Olympic gold at age 15 in St. Moritz, transforming ladies' skating with balletic style.

name: women-olympic-gymnastics-debut-1928
description: Women competed in Olympic gymnastics for the first time at the Amsterdam Games, where the host Netherlands won the team event.

name: first-monaco-grand-prix-1929
description: William Grover-Williams won the first Monaco Grand Prix in a Bugatti T35B on the streets of Monte Carlo.

name: bobby-jones-grand-slam-1930
description: Amateur Bobby Jones won all four majors of his era in one calendar year, golf's only single-season Grand Slam.

name: phar-lap-melbourne-cup-1930
description: Australian champion Phar Lap won the 1930 Melbourne Cup as the shortest-priced favourite in the race's history.

name: first-british-empire-games-1930
description: The first British Empire Games, forerunner of the Commonwealth Games, were held in Hamilton, Canada.

name: world-archery-fita-founded-1931
description: Seven nations founded FITA, now World Archery, in Lwów to govern the sport and return it to the Olympic programme.

name: bodyline-series-1932
description: England's 1932-33 Bodyline tactics of fast leg-side bowling to curb Don Bradman sparked a major diplomatic row.

name: first-masters-tournament-1934
description: Horton Smith won the inaugural Masters at Augusta National, the Georgia course co-founded by Bobby Jones.

name: trampoline-invented-nissen-1934
description: American gymnast George Nissen and coach Larry Griswold built the first modern trampoline from iron, canvas and rubber.

name: badminton-federation-founded-1934
description: Nine nations founded the International Badminton Federation, now the Badminton World Federation, the sport's global governing body.

name: first-heisman-trophy-1935
description: Chicago halfback Jay Berwanger won the first Downtown Athletic Club Trophy, renamed the Heisman Trophy the next year.

name: first-vuelta-a-espana-1935-deloor
description: Belgian Gustaaf Deloor won the inaugural Vuelta a España, a 14-stage race finishing in Madrid.

name: owens-six-records-ann-arbor-1935
description: Jesse Owens set five world records and tied a sixth in 45 minutes at the Big Ten meet in Ann Arbor, Michigan.

name: campbell-tops-300-mph-1935
description: Malcolm Campbell drove his Bluebird past 300 mph at Bonneville Salt Flats, the first person to reach that speed on land.

name: basketball-olympic-debut-1936
description: Basketball debuted as an Olympic medal event at the 1936 Berlin Games, with the USA winning gold on an outdoor court.

name: alpine-skiing-olympic-debut-1936
description: Alpine skiing debuted at the Olympics at Garmisch-Partenkirchen in 1936 with a combined downhill and slalom event.

name: first-olympic-torch-relay-1936
description: The Berlin Games introduced the Olympic torch relay, carrying a flame lit in Olympia, Greece, to the host stadium.

name: japanese-pro-baseball-league-1936
description: The Japanese Baseball League launched with seven teams, establishing professional baseball in Japan for the first time.

name: first-televised-nfl-game
description: NBC broadcast the first televised NFL game as the Brooklyn Dodgers beat the Philadelphia Eagles at Ebbets Field.

name: baseball-hall-of-fame-opens-1939
description: The National Baseball Hall of Fame and Museum opened in Cooperstown, New York, honoring its first inducted class.

name: first-ncaa-basketball-tournament
description: The inaugural NCAA men's basketball tournament was held, with Oregon defeating Ohio State to claim the first national title.

name: rocket-richard-50-goals-50-games-1945
description: Maurice 'Rocket' Richard became the first NHL player to score 50 goals in a season, doing it in just 50 games.

name: bradman-final-test-duck-1948
description: Don Bradman was bowled for a duck in his final Test innings in 1948, leaving his career batting average at 99.94.

name: nascar-founded-1948
description: Bill France Sr. founded the National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing in Daytona Beach, Florida, on February 21.

name: first-stoke-mandeville-games-1948
description: Dr Ludwig Guttmann staged the Stoke Mandeville Games for wheelchair veterans in England, seeding the Paralympic movement.

name: nba-formed-baa-nbl-merger
description: The NBA was created on August 3, 1949, when the Basketball Association of America merged with the National Basketball League.

name: first-thomas-cup-1949
description: Malaysia won the inaugural Thomas Cup, the world men's team badminton championship donated by former champion George Thomas.

name: first-motorcycle-gp-world-championship-1949
description: The FIM launched the first motorcycle road racing world championship, the oldest of all motorsport world titles.

name: coppi-giro-tour-double-1949
description: Fausto Coppi became the first rider to win the Giro d'Italia and Tour de France in the same year in 1949.

name: first-f1-world-championship-race-1950
description: Nino Farina won the first Formula One World Championship race at Silverstone in an Alfa Romeo, watched by King George VI.

name: first-fiba-world-championship-1950
description: Argentina beat the United States to win the first FIBA World Championship, hosted in Buenos Aires in 1950.

name: the-maracanazo-1950
description: Uruguay stunned host Brazil 2-1 before a record Maracanã crowd to win the World Cup in Rio de Janeiro.

name: first-asian-games-1951
description: New Delhi hosted the inaugural Asian Games in 1951, with 489 athletes from 11 nations competing across eight sports.

name: first-pan-american-games-1951
description: Buenos Aires staged the first Pan American Games in 1951, gathering over 2,500 athletes from 21 nations of the Americas.

name: shot-heard-round-the-world-1951
description: Bobby Thomson's walk-off pennant-winning home run for the Giants over the Dodgers became known as the Shot Heard Round the World.

name: zatopek-helsinki-triple-1952
description: Emil Zatopek won the 5000m, 10000m and marathon at the 1952 Helsinki Olympics, an unmatched distance triple.

name: dick-button-first-triple-jump-1952
description: American Dick Button landed the first triple jump in figure skating competition at the Oslo Winter Olympics.

name: lis-hartel-dressage-silver-1952
description: Denmark's Lis Hartel took dressage silver at the 1952 Helsinki Olympics, the first woman to win an Olympic equestrian medal.

name: hungary-beat-england-1953
description: Hungary's Mighty Magyars thrashed England 6-3 at Wembley, the first foreign side to beat England at home.

name: hogan-triple-major-year-1953
description: Ben Hogan won the Masters, U.S. Open and Open Championship in a single season, a feat later called the Hogan Slam.

name: butterfly-separate-stroke-1953
description: FINA recognized butterfly as a distinct competitive stroke, splitting it from breaststroke after the 1952 Olympics.

name: nba-24-second-shot-clock-introduced
description: The NBA introduced Danny Biasone's 24-second shot clock for the 1954-55 season, transforming the pace of the game.

name: taekwondo-is-named-1955
description: General Choi Hong Hi coined the name taekwondo in South Korea, unifying Korean kicking-and-striking martial arts.

name: le-mans-disaster-1955
description: Pierre Levegh's Mercedes crashed into the crowd at Le Mans, killing over 80 spectators in motorsport's deadliest accident.

name: first-european-cup-1956
description: Real Madrid beat Stade de Reims 4-3 in Paris to win the first European Cup, Europe's premier club competition.

name: laker-nineteen-wickets-1956
description: Jim Laker took a record 19 wickets in the 1956 Old Trafford Test against Australia, a feat unmatched in Test cricket.

name: blood-in-the-water-1956
description: Hungary beat the USSR 4-0 in a violent Olympic water polo semi-final weeks after the crushed Hungarian Revolution.

name: larsen-world-series-perfect-game-1956
description: Yankee Don Larsen pitched the only perfect game in World Series history, beating the Brooklyn Dodgers in Game 5.

name: first-uber-cup-1957
description: The first Uber Cup, the women's world team badminton championship proposed by Betty Uber, was won by the United States.

name: fangio-fifth-f1-title-1957
description: Juan Manuel Fangio clinched his fifth Formula One title, a record that stood for 46 years until Michael Schumacher.

name: munich-air-disaster-1958
description: A plane crash in Munich killed eight Manchester United players, devastating Matt Busby's celebrated young side.

name: greatest-game-ever-played
description: The Baltimore Colts beat the New York Giants in sudden-death overtime for the NFL title in a game credited with popularizing pro football.

name: afl-founded-lamar-hunt-1959
description: Lamar Hunt founded the American Football League with eight teams to rival the NFL, beginning play in the 1960 season.

name: first-daytona-500-1959
description: The first Daytona 500 ran at the new Daytona International Speedway, with Lee Petty declared winner after a photo finish.

name: first-european-nations-cup-1960
description: The Soviet Union won the first European Nations' Cup in 1960, beating Yugoslavia 2-1 in the final in Paris.

name: abebe-bikila-barefoot-marathon-1960
description: Ethiopia's Abebe Bikila won the Rome Olympic marathon running barefoot, the first sub-Saharan African Olympic champion.

name: first-tied-test-1960
description: The first tied Test came in 1960 at Brisbane, where Australia and the West Indies finished level after a dramatic finish.

name: wilt-chamberlain-100-points
description: Wilt Chamberlain scored 100 points in a single NBA game for the Philadelphia Warriors against the New York Knicks.

name: fraser-sub-minute-1962
friendly_name: Fraser First Woman Under a Minute
description: Dawn Fraser became the first woman to break one minute for 100m freestyle, swimming 59.9 in Melbourne.

name: first-rhythmic-gymnastics-worlds-1963
description: The first Rhythmic Gymnastics World Championships were held in Budapest, won by Soviet gymnast Ludmila Savinkova.

name: judo-olympic-debut-tokyo-1964
description: Judo made its Olympic debut in Tokyo, where Dutchman Anton Geesink upset the hosts to win the open weight gold.

name: latynina-most-olympic-medals-1964
description: Soviet gymnast Larisa Latynina ended her career with 18 Olympic medals, a record for any athlete that stood for 48 years.

name: luge-olympic-debut-1964
description: Luge made its Olympic debut at the Innsbruck Winter Games with men's and women's singles and doubles events.

name: first-all-africa-games-1965
description: The first All-Africa Games were held in Brazzaville, Congo, drawing around 2,500 athletes from 30 African nations.

name: england-win-world-cup-1966
description: England beat West Germany 4-2 after extra time at Wembley, with Geoff Hurst scoring a famous hat-trick.

name: celtics-eighth-straight-title-1966
description: The Bill Russell-led Boston Celtics won their eighth consecutive NBA championship, a record dynasty in North American sports.

name: ford-gt40-sweeps-le-mans-1966
description: Ford's GT40 Mk II took a 1-2-3 finish at Le Mans, ending Ferrari's dominance and giving America its first overall win.

name: ice-bowl
description: Green Bay beat Dallas for the NFL title at minus-13 degrees in Green Bay, one of the coldest games in football history.

name: tom-simpson-ventoux-death-1967
description: British champion Tom Simpson collapsed and died climbing Mont Ventoux during the 1967 Tour de France, amphetamines in his system.

name: black-power-salute-mexico-1968
description: Tommie Smith and John Carlos raised black-gloved fists on the Mexico City podium to protest racial injustice.

name: tennis-open-era-begins-1968
description: In 1968 tennis entered the Open era, letting professionals and amateurs compete together at the Grand Slam tournaments.

name: fosbury-flop-high-jump-1968
description: Dick Fosbury won Olympic high jump gold with his revolutionary backwards Fosbury Flop, transforming the event.

name: beamon-long-jump-leap-1968
description: Bob Beamon long jumped 8.90m at the Mexico City Olympics, shattering the world record by nearly 55 centimetres.

name: killy-triple-gold-1968
description: France's Jean-Claude Killy swept all three Alpine skiing gold medals at the Grenoble Winter Olympics.

name: super-bowl-iii-jets-upset
description: Joe Namath backed up his guarantee as the AFL's New York Jets upset the heavily favored Baltimore Colts 16-7.

name: laver-calendar-grand-slam-1969
description: Rod Laver won all four majors in one year, becoming the only man to complete a calendar-year Grand Slam in the Open era.

name: afl-nfl-merger-1970
description: The AFL and NFL completed their merger into one league split into two conferences, the AFC and NFC, for the 1970 season.

name: monday-night-football-debut
description: ABC aired the first Monday Night Football broadcast, with the Cleveland Browns hosting the New York Jets in prime time.

name: red-yellow-cards-debut-1970
description: Colour-coded yellow and red cards were introduced at the 1970 World Cup in Mexico to clarify disciplinary decisions.

name: alekseyev-600kg-total-1970
description: Soviet weightlifter Vasily Alekseyev became the first man to total 600 kg across the three lifts, starting a run of 80 world records.

name: first-one-day-international-1971
description: After a washed-out Test, Australia beat England at Melbourne in the first One Day International, watched by 46,000 fans.

name: golf-played-on-the-moon-1971
description: Apollo 14 astronaut Alan Shepard hit two golf balls on the lunar surface, the first time golf was played off Earth.

name: ali-frazier-fight-of-century
description: Joe Frazier beat Muhammad Ali by decision in the 'Fight of the Century,' the first bout between undefeated heavyweight champions.

name: lions-series-win-new-zealand-1971
friendly_name: Lions Win Series in New Zealand
description: The British and Irish Lions won their only Test series in New Zealand, a landmark result over the All Blacks.

name: spitz-seven-golds-1972
description: Mark Spitz won seven gold medals at the 1972 Munich Olympics, each in world-record time, a record that stood 36 years.

name: immaculate-reception
description: Franco Harris scooped a deflected pass off his shoetops to score a last-second playoff touchdown for the Pittsburgh Steelers.

name: summit-series-henderson-goal-1972
description: Paul Henderson scored late in Game 8 to win the 1972 Summit Series for Canada over the Soviet Union.

name: korbut-flip-munich-1972
description: Olga Korbut performed the first backward release move on the uneven bars at the Munich Olympics, later named the Korbut Flip.

name: merckx-hour-record-1972
description: Eddy Merckx rode 49.431 km at altitude in Mexico City in 1972 to set a legendary hour record that stood for years.

name: secretariat-triple-crown-1973
description: Secretariat swept the 1973 US Triple Crown, winning the Belmont Stakes by a record 31 lengths.

name: gareth-edwards-greatest-try
description: Gareth Edwards finished a flowing Barbarians move against the All Blacks, often called rugby's greatest try.

name: world-rally-championship-founded-1973
description: The World Rally Championship debuted with the Monte Carlo Rally, and Alpine-Renault won the first manufacturers' title.

name: first-world-champs-1973
description: The inaugural World Aquatics Championships were held in Belgrade, gathering swimmers, divers and water polo from 47 nations.

name: hank-aaron-715th-home-run-1974
description: Atlanta's Hank Aaron hit his 715th home run off Al Downing, surpassing Babe Ruth as baseball's career home run king.

name: rumble-in-the-jungle
description: Muhammad Ali knocked out George Foreman in Kinshasa, Zaire, reclaiming the heavyweight title in the 'Rumble in the Jungle.'

name: first-cricket-world-cup-1975
description: West Indies beat Australia at Lord's to win the first men's Cricket World Cup, the Prudential Cup, with Clive Lloyd scoring 102.

name: first-squash-world-open-1976
description: Geoff Hunt won the first men's World Open in London, the tournament that became squash's world individual championship.

name: red-rum-third-grand-national-1977
description: Red Rum won a record third Grand National in 1977, cementing his status as Aintree's most beloved steeplechaser.

name: sadaharu-oh-756th-home-run-1977
description: Japan's Sadaharu Oh hit his 756th career home run, passing Hank Aaron to become the world's all-time home run king.

name: first-ironman-triathlon-1978
description: Fifteen athletes raced a swim, bike and run on Oahu in the first Ironman; Gordon Haller won in under twelve hours.

name: nba-three-point-line-adopted
description: The NBA introduced the three-point line for the 1979-80 season, with Chris Ford making the league's first three-pointer.

name: magic-bird-ncaa-final-1979
description: Magic Johnson's Michigan State beat Larry Bird's Indiana State in the highest-rated NCAA title game ever televised.

name: eric-heiden-five-golds-1980
description: American Eric Heiden won all five speed skating events at the Lake Placid Olympics, setting records in each.

name: moscow-olympics-boycott-1980
description: Over 60 nations led by the United States boycotted the Moscow Summer Olympics over the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan.

name: first-state-of-origin-match
description: Queensland beat New South Wales in the first State of Origin game, launching Australia's fierce rugby league series.

name: gretzky-92-goal-season-1982
description: Wayne Gretzky set the NHL single-season record with 92 goals for the Edmonton Oilers in 1981-82.

name: kbo-league-founded-korea-1982
description: South Korea's professional KBO League launched with six franchises, opening its inaugural season in Seoul.

name: first-televised-147-break-1982
description: Steve Davis compiled snooker's first televised maximum 147 break at the Lada Classic, winning a car for the feat.

name: india-world-cup-1983
description: India stunned the West Indies at Lord's to win the 1983 World Cup, transforming cricket's popularity across India.

name: australia-ii-wins-americas-cup-1983
description: John Bertrand's Australia II ended the New York Yacht Club's 132-year hold on the America's Cup, sailing's longest streak.

name: kareem-scoring-record-1984
description: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar passed Wilt Chamberlain to become the NBA's all-time leading scorer with a signature skyhook.

name: first-womens-olympic-marathon-1984
description: Joan Benoit of the USA won the first women's Olympic marathon at the Los Angeles Games in 1984.

name: torvill-dean-bolero-1984
description: Britain's Torvill and Dean earned perfect artistic scores skating to Ravel's Bolero at the Sarajevo Olympics.

name: rhythmic-gymnastics-olympic-debut-1984
description: Rhythmic gymnastics debuted at the Los Angeles Olympics, where Canada's Lori Fung won the first all-around gold medal.

name: sync-swim-olympic-1984
description: Synchronized swimming made its Olympic debut at the 1984 Los Angeles Games with solo and duet events for women.

name: snooker-black-ball-final-1985
description: Dennis Taylor beat Steve Davis on the final black to win the World Championship before 18.5 million late-night viewers.

name: maradona-hand-of-god-1986
description: Diego Maradona scored the Hand of God and Goal of the Century against England en route to Argentina's World Cup win.

name: nicklaus-masters-at-46-1986
description: Jack Nicklaus won the Masters at age 46 to claim a record 18th major, becoming the oldest Masters champion.

name: lemond-first-us-tour-win-1986
description: Greg LeMond became the first American to win the Tour de France in 1986, beating teammate Bernard Hinault.

name: first-rugby-world-cup-1987
description: New Zealand's All Blacks beat France in Auckland to win the first Rugby World Cup, co-hosted by New Zealand and Australia.

name: cox-bering-strait-1987
description: Lynne Cox swam the icy Bering Strait from Alaska to a Soviet island, helping thaw Cold War tensions.

name: table-tennis-olympic-debut-1988
description: Table tennis made its Olympic debut at the Seoul Games, with China and South Korea sharing the first gold medals.

name: graf-golden-slam-1988
description: Steffi Graf won all four majors and Olympic gold in one year, the only Golden Slam in tennis history.

name: flo-jo-100m-world-record-1988
description: Florence Griffith-Joyner ran a stunning 10.49 for the 100m, setting a women's world record that still stands.

name: louganis-head-injury-1988
description: Greg Louganis struck his head on the springboard yet came back to win Olympic diving gold at the 1988 Seoul Games.

name: suleymanoglu-seoul-triple-1988
description: Turkey's Naim Suleymanoglu set three world records at Seoul, clean and jerking 190 kg at over three times his body weight.

name: lemond-eight-second-win-1989
description: Greg LeMond overturned a 50-second deficit on the final time trial to beat Laurent Fignon by eight seconds in the 1989 Tour de France.

name: first-vendee-globe-1989
description: Titouan Lamazou won the inaugural Vendée Globe, the solo, non-stop, unassisted round-the-world yacht race, in 109 days.

name: first-solheim-cup-1990
description: The inaugural Solheim Cup, women's golf's answer to the Ryder Cup, was won by the United States at Lake Nona, Florida.

name: first-womens-world-cup-1991
description: The United States beat Norway 2-1 in China to win the inaugural FIFA Women's World Cup football tournament.

name: short-track-olympic-debut-1992
description: Short track speed skating made its official Olympic debut at the Albertville Winter Games with four medal events.

name: dream-team-1992-olympics
description: The USA Dream Team of NBA stars including Jordan, Magic, and Bird dominated the Barcelona Olympics to win gold.

name: badminton-olympic-debut-1992
description: Badminton made its Olympic debut at the Barcelona Games, with Indonesia winning both singles gold medals.

name: first-ufc-event-1993
description: UFC 1 took place in Denver, Colorado, where Brazilian Royce Gracie won the no-rules eight-man tournament.

name: akebono-first-foreign-yokozuna
description: Hawaiian-born Akebono was promoted to yokozuna, becoming the first foreign-born grand champion in sumo's history.

name: lara-375-test-record-1994
description: Brian Lara scored 375 against England at Antigua in 1994, breaking Garfield Sobers' 36-year-old Test batting record.

name: kerrigan-harding-attack-1994
description: US skater Nancy Kerrigan was clubbed in a plot linked to rival Tonya Harding's camp weeks before the Olympics.

name: first-presidents-cup-1994
description: The first Presidents Cup was held in Virginia, with the U.S. team beating an International side of non-European players.

name: first-extreme-games-1995-rhode-island
description: ESPN staged the first Extreme Games in Rhode Island, launching the action-sports showcase later renamed the X Games.

name: mandela-1995-springboks
description: South Africa won the Rugby World Cup on home soil as Nelson Mandela donned a Springbok jersey to unite the nation.

name: rugby-union-turns-professional
description: The IRB declared rugby union an open game, ending amateurism a century after the split that created rugby league.

name: ripken-breaks-iron-man-record-1995
description: Cal Ripken Jr. played his 2,131st consecutive game, breaking Lou Gehrig's long-standing durability record.

name: bosman-ruling-1995
description: A European court ruling let players move freely at contract's end, transforming transfers and player wages in football.

name: olympic-softball-debut-1996
description: Women's fast-pitch softball debuted at the Atlanta Olympics, with the United States winning the first gold medal.

name: wnba-inaugural-season-1997
description: The Women's National Basketball Association tipped off its first season in 1997, launching a lasting US women's pro league.

name: tyson-bites-holyfields-ear
description: Mike Tyson was disqualified for biting Evander Holyfield's ear in one of boxing's most infamous heavyweight bouts.

name: thrustssc-breaks-sound-barrier-1997
description: Andy Green drove ThrustSSC to 763 mph in Nevada's Black Rock Desert, the first land vehicle to break the sound barrier.

name: jordan-sixth-title-1998
friendly_name: Jordan's Last Shot for Sixth Title
description: Michael Jordan hit a jumper over Bryon Russell to clinch the Chicago Bulls' sixth NBA championship of the decade.

name: snowboarding-olympic-debut-1998
description: Snowboarding made its Olympic debut at the Nagano Winter Games with halfpipe and giant slalom events.

name: italy-joins-six-nations-2000
description: Italy joined the Five Nations to create the Six Nations Championship, beating Scotland in their debut match in Rome.

name: taekwondo-becomes-olympic-sport-2000
description: Taekwondo became a full Olympic medal sport at the Sydney Games, with eight weight divisions for men and women.

name: tiger-pebble-beach-us-open-2000
description: Tiger Woods won the U.S. Open at Pebble Beach by a record 15 strokes, the largest winning margin in any men's major.

name: redgrave-fifth-rowing-gold-2000
description: Steve Redgrave won gold in the coxless four at Sydney, becoming the only rower with five golds at five straight Olympics.

name: first-twenty20-match-2003
description: Hampshire beat Sussex at the Rose Bowl in the first Twenty20 match, launching England's inaugural Twenty20 Cup competition.

name: england-win-rugby-world-cup-2003
description: Jonny Wilkinson's extra-time drop goal beat Australia to win England their first Rugby Union World Cup.

name: first-kabaddi-world-cup-india
description: India beat Iran 55-27 in Mumbai to win the inaugural Kabaddi World Cup, cementing its dominance of the international game.

name: red-sox-end-86-year-drought-2004
description: The Boston Red Sox swept the World Series to win their first title since 1918, ending the Curse of the Bambino.

name: kobe-81-point-game-2006
description: Kobe Bryant scored 81 points against the Toronto Raptors, the second-highest single-game total in NBA history.

name: patriots-perfect-regular-season
description: The New England Patriots completed the NFL's first 16-0 regular season before losing to the Giants in Super Bowl XLII.

name: first-ipl-season-2008
description: The Indian Premier League's inaugural franchise season in 2008 reshaped cricket's economy, with Rajasthan Royals champions.

name: phelps-eight-golds-2008
description: Michael Phelps won a record eight gold medals at the 2008 Beijing Olympics, surpassing Mark Spitz's mark of seven.

name: womens-olympic-boxing-debut-2012
description: Women's boxing debuted at the London Olympics, where Britain's Nicola Adams won the first female Olympic gold.

name: armstrong-stripped-titles-2012
description: Lance Armstrong was stripped of his seven Tour de France titles and banned for life in 2012 after a US doping investigation.

name: frankel-retires-unbeaten-2012
description: Unbeaten in all 14 starts, the British colt Frankel retired in 2012 as the highest-rated racehorse in the world.

name: nyad-cuba-florida-2013
description: At 64, Diana Nyad became the first to swim from Cuba to Florida without a shark cage, a 53-hour crossing.

name: first-college-football-playoff
description: The four-team College Football Playoff debuted for the 2014 season, replacing the BCS, with Ohio State winning the first title.

name: ledecky-1500-wr-2015
description: Katie Ledecky smashed the 1500m freestyle world record at the 2015 Kazan Worlds, extending her long-distance reign.

name: american-pharoah-triple-crown-2015
description: American Pharoah won the 2015 Belmont Stakes to claim the first US Triple Crown in 37 years since Affirmed.

name: biles-four-rio-golds-2016
description: Simone Biles won four gymnastics gold medals at the Rio Olympics, taking the team, all-around, vault and floor titles.

name: super-bowl-li-comeback
description: The New England Patriots erased a 28-3 deficit to beat the Atlanta Falcons in the first Super Bowl decided in overtime.

name: var-world-cup-2018
description: Video Assistant Referee technology was used for the first time at a FIFA World Cup, held in Russia.

name: world-cup-super-over-final-2019
description: England won the 2019 World Cup final over New Zealand on boundary count after the match and super over finished level.

name: tiger-masters-comeback-2019
description: Tiger Woods won the Masters for his 15th major, an emotional comeback 11 years after his previous major title.

name: kipchoge-sub-two-hour-marathon-2019
description: Eliud Kipchoge ran 1:59:40 in Vienna, the first sub-two-hour marathon, though not an official world record.

name: winx-33rd-straight-win-2019
description: Australian mare Winx won her 33rd consecutive race in her 2019 farewell, a modern record for a top-level horse.

name: action-sports-olympic-debut-2021
description: Skateboarding, surfing and sport climbing all made their Olympic debut at the Tokyo Games held in 2021.

name: lebron-nba-scoring-record-2023
description: LeBron James passed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to become the NBA's all-time leading scorer with a fadeaway jumper.
